article_title: Obligations for providers of general-purpose AI models
obligation_text: Providers of general-purpose AI models shall draw up, keep up-to-date and make available information and documentation to providers of AI systems who intend to integrate the general-purpose AI model into their AI systems, to enable them to have a good understanding of the capabilities and limitations of the general-purpose AI model and to comply with their obligations.
scoring_text: Skill in creating downstream integration documentation for general-purpose AI models, including capability and limitation disclosure, integration guidance for AI system providers, and documentation that enables downstream regulatory compliance.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in creating downstream integration documentation for general-purpose AI models, including capability and limitation disclosure, integration guidance for AI system providers, and documentation that enables downstream regulatory compliance.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in developing AI system documentation including model cards, system cards, and datasheets for datasets that meet both internal governance review and external regulatory conformity assessment requirements.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 53(1)(b) (obligation) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-S-008 is the strongest match (STS: 0.787, cosine: 0.773). Secondary: RM-T-004 (RM, cosine: 0.737), RM-K-008 (RM, cosine: 0.723).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.9/10 (raw 0.787). Shared vocabulary: [ai, documentation, regulatory, skill, system]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [capability, compliance, creating, disclosure, downstream, enables] vs WIDAI terms [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.894 (P=0.899, R=0.889). Balanced P/R — the 28-word competency implication and 27-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.9%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.773 ≈ STS 0.787 (gap 0.013). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, documentation, regulatory, skill, system] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 27.0%. Shared tokens: [ai, documentation, regulatory, skill, system]. EU AI Act-only: [capability, compliance, creating, disclosure, downstream, enables]. WIDAI-only: [assessment, cards, conformity, datasets, datasheets, developing].
